n^cn
In the north lie the Kailasa, Himavat, Vasumat, Dhanusmat,
Krauftca, Meru (all mountains), the Uttara Kuru country, the
Ksudramtnas, Kaikayas, Vasatis, Yamunas, Bhogaprastha, the
Arjunayanas, Agnidhras, Adarsas, Antardvipins, Trigarta, the
Turagananas (horse-faced people), the ^vamukhas (dog-faced
people), Kesadharas (the hairy ones) Gipitanasikas (flat-nosed tribe)
Daserakas, Vatadhanas, Saradhanas, Taxila, Puskalavata, Kailavata,
Kanthadhanas, Ambaravatas, Madrakas, Malwa, Pauravas, Kacch-
aras, Dandapingalakas, Manahalas, Huns, Kohalas, Sitakas,
Mandavyas, Bhutapura, Gandhara, Yasovati (town), Hematalas,
Ksatriyas, Khacaras, Gavyas, the Yaudheyas, Dasameyas, Syama-
kas and Ksemadhurtas. These are ruled by the triad of stars begin-
ning with Satabhisaj,
[The mountain Krauftca has been identified with that part of
the Kailasa mountain on which Lake Manasa is situated.
Meru is identified by some with a part of the Himalayas.
